channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ontario and B.C. Limit the Disclosure of Full Names for Lottery Winners
item.description: In recent years, the Ontario Lottery and Gaming Corporation (OLG) and the British Columbia Lottery Corporation (BCLC) have revised their policies on disclosing the identities of lottery winners. The p...

item.title: Tanzanian PhD Student Ngasuma Wins Harvard Leadership Award
item.description: Former President Jakaya Kikwete has congratulated Tanzanian PhD student Ms Ngasuma Kanyeka for earning a prestigious award at Harvard University. The award, given under the Harvard Ministerial Leaders...

item.title: Three Police Officers Killed in Early Morning Bandit Attack in Kwara
item.description: Early Saturday morning, bandits launched a deadly attack on the Police Mobile Force camp located in Tenebo community, Kaiama LGA, Kwara State. The attack, which happened around 3 a.m., left three poli...

item.title: Germany reacts to US troop withdrawal amid ongoing Iran conflict
item.description: Germany has acknowledged that the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from its territory was foreseeable, as tensions between the United States and Europe over the Iran conflict continue to grow. German Def...

item.title: Yanis Varoufakis Annotates and Critiques Palantir's 22-Point Manifesto
item.description: In this opinion piece, economist and former Greek Finance Minister Yanis Varoufakis provides a sharp, point-by-point critique of what he describes as Palantir's 'hideous ideology' summarized in 22 poi...

item.title: Tanzania and India Urged to Strengthen Trade Cooperation Through JTC
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and India have been encouraged to make full use of the Joint Trade Committee (JTC) as a practical way to tackle trade obstacles and boost economic cooperation. Ambassador Dr S...

item.title: President Ruto Announces Wage Increase Amid Worker Protests
item.description: On Labour Day 2026, President William Ruto announced a salary adjustment aimed at easing the financial strain on Kenyan workers. The government will increase general wages by 12 percent, while agricul...

item.title: Philippines and allies deploy 11 vessels for joint maritime drills in West Philippine Sea as part of Balikatan 2026 exercises
item.description: In a show of strengthened alliances, the Philippines together with its like-minded partners from the United States, Japan, Australia, and Canada deployed a total of 11 vessels for joint maritime drill...

item.title: Terrorists Attack PMF Camp in Kwara State, Kill Three Officers and Injure Two Others
item.description: Suspected terrorists launch early morning attack on Police Mobile Force camp for Tenebo community inside Kaiama Local Government Area of Kwara State. Na around 3am on Saturday wey di heavily armed men...

item.title: Government Directs Councils to Protect Business Spaces for Traders with Disabilities
item.description: Dodoma — The Tanzanian government has instructed local authorities to strictly enforce regulations that protect business spaces meant for persons with disabilities. Deputy Minister Festo Dugange addre...

item.title: Mo Salah opens up about his decision to leave Liverpool at the end of the season
item.description: In an exclusive interview with Stan Sport, Liverpool's star player Mo Salah has revealed that he is 'at peace' with his decision to leave the club at the end of the current season. The Egyptian forwar...

item.title: Nine Suspected Gang Members Arrested During Joint DCI and Police Operation in Trans Nzoia West
item.description: In a swift security operation carried out on 1 May 2026, Kenyan authorities arrested nine suspected members of a criminal gang in Trans Nzoia West Sub-County. The joint team from the Directorate of Cr...

item.title: Minister Manamela details timelines and funding challenges for proposed Ekurhuleni University of Science and Innovation
item.description: Higher Education Minister Buti Manamela has provided an update on the proposed Ekurhuleni University of Science and Innovation, indicating that construction cannot start until feasibility studies are ...

item.title: Challenges and Opportunities in India’s Shift to Renewable Energy
item.description: The latest episode of Eco India, Episode 320, delves into the ongoing transition towards renewable energy in India. The programme explores both the opportunities and the obstacles that the country fac...

item.title: India and Ecuador discuss preferential trade agreement and bilateral cooperation
item.description: India and Ecuador are exploring the possibility of formalising a preferential trade agreement to strengthen economic and bilateral relations between the two nations. During a two-day visit to India, E...

item.title: Mother Alleges Househelp Harmed Child Following Salary Dispute in Nairobi
item.description: A mother from Ruai, Nairobi, Ann Wanjiru, is mourning the tragic death of her four-year-old daughter, whom she suspects was harmed by her househelp, Joyce Onyango, after a disagreement over pay. Joyce...

item.title: Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi: A Shift Towards African-Led Partnerships with France
item.description: The Africa Forward Summit, set for Nairobi on 11 and 12 May 2026, marks a significant change in Africa-France relations as France comes to African soil for the first time in this summit format. The ar...

item.title: School District Supervisor Killed in Zamboanga Sibugay Attack
item.description: A school district supervisor, Rey Camaingking, was shot and killed on Thursday night in Alicia, Zamboanga Sibugay. He was walking towards the Guicam port in Barangay Dawa-Dawa when two unidentified as...

item.title: Wide Crack Spread in Diesel Pricing Allows Refineries Windfall Profits Despite Formula Revision
item.description: According to detailed analysis of official data from March and April 2026, local refineries in Pakistan have earned huge extra profits because of wide gap between international diesel prices and crude...

item.title: Justice V K Mohanan Commission submits 141 safety recommendations after Tanur boat tragedy
item.description: The Justice V K Mohanan Commission of Inquiry, set up to investigate the tragic Tanur boat accident of May 7, 2023 in Malappuram district of Kerala, has submitted its final 14-volume report to Chief M...
